item.text:
- evidence used
  - `specs/customer-feature.md:3`: spec status is `approved`
  - `specs/customer-feature.md:13`: R1 requires local source/test files
  - `specs/customer-feature.md:15`: R2 says fixture must not require RigorLoop internals
  - `docs/plans/customer-plan.md:5`: current milestone is `Fixture milestone`
  - `docs/plans/customer-plan.md:6`: milestone state is `planned`
  - `docs/plans/customer-plan.md:8`: next stage is `implement fixture milestone`
  - `src/app.js:1`: `greeting(name)` currently accepts only `name`
  - `src/app.js:2`: output is currently `hello ${name}`
  - `tests/app.test.js:4`: test covers `greeting("customer") === "hello customer"`

- whether local `docs/workflows.md` was used
  - yes; `docs/workflows.md:5-11` confirms customer-project portable defaults and no reliance on RigorLoop internals; `docs/workflows.md:13-19` confirms local artifact locations

- portable default or ambiguity behavior
  - portable default: use only local artifacts and local source/tests
  - ambiguity behavior: adding a greeting suffix option is not currently specified in `specs/customer-feature.md`, so the implementation milestone should be proposed as blocked on a spec update or explicit acceptance criteria for suffix behavior

- attempted reliance on absent RigorLoop internals: yes/no
  - no

- current local plan state
  - `docs/plans/customer-plan.md` has one planned milestone: `Fixture milestone`
  - current handoff says final closeout is not ready
  - no suffix-specific milestone exists yet

- proposed next milestone
  - `M2. Add greeting suffix option`
  - Milestone state: `planned`
  - Goal: extend `greeting(name)` to support an optional suffix while preserving the existing default output
  - Requirements: blocked until suffix behavior is added to the local spec or explicitly approved
  - Files likely touched: `src/app.js`, `tests/app.test.js`, and likely `specs/customer-feature.md` before implementation
  - Tests to add/update: keep existing default greeting test; add suffix test such as `greeting("customer", "!") === "hello customer!"`
  - Validation command: run the local JS test command once identified from project tooling; if no runner exists, execute `node tests/app.test.js`
  - Expected observable result: existing callers still get `hello customer`; callers passing a suffix get the suffix appended
  - Next stage: update/approve spec for suffix behavior before implementation, or explicitly authorize this isolated implementation despite the spec gap
